opportunities.title: Godzilla Doesn't Get One Turn: Fixing Kaiju Action Economy in 5e (Legendary Actions the DIY Way)
opportunities.why_now: Marvel Multiverse RPG just announced a Godzilla stat block, giving a hook to break down how to run a genuinely threatening giant monster without a Legendary Actions license.
opportunities.angle: 60-sec creature tactic: house-rule a 'Legendary Actions' patch for Shadowdark/DCC where the system has no native equivalent, using Godzilla as the worked example.

opportunities.title: Deathbringer's Death Saves Are Meaner Than Yours — Should They Be?
opportunities.why_now: Professor Dungeon Master's interview on Deathbringer surfaces its grimmer death-and-dying subsystem right as it's mid-crowdfund, a natural comparison point.
opportunities.angle: Rules ruling: side-by-side dry breakdown of 5e death saves vs Deathbringer's harsher take vs Shadowdark's 1-HP-and-done, then a recommendation for which table type each suits.

opportunities.title: Your Background Does Nothing. Arcana Unleashed's Arcane Factions Actually Do Something.
opportunities.why_now: The fresh preview of Arcana Unleashed's faction/background material lands right as GMs are fatigued with 5e backgrounds being pure flavor text.
opportunities.angle: Spell/mechanic use-case: pick one previewed faction perk, show the exact mechanical interaction (advantage/resource/contact), and rule on whether it's worth porting into a home 5e game.

opportunities.title: Gary's Appendix: The OSR Crowdfund Your Shadowdark Table Should Be Watching
opportunities.why_now: Gary's Appendix just surfaced in this week's RPG crowdfunding roundup alongside Deathbringer and Sly Flourish, squarely in OSR/Appendix N territory that overlaps DCC's lineage.
opportunities.angle: Quick-hit news/creature-tactic tease: pull one monster or procedure teased in the campaign and give a one-line verdict on whether it slots into DCC/Shadowdark as-is.

opportunities.title: Monte Cook Is Turning Novels Into a TTRPG — Here's the One Mechanic Worth Stealing for 5e
opportunities.why_now: The Between Earth and Sky announcement gives a rare timely peg to talk about non-d20 magic/resource systems in a way that's directly actionable for a 5e/Shadowdark audience.
opportunities.angle: Rules ruling framed as 'steal this': identify one narrative mechanic from the Cypher-adjacent system and give the exact reskin/houserule to bolt it onto 5e spellcasting.
